title: Fix event feed dropping events after 24 hours
glance: Events were saved in Postgres but disappeared from the app about a day after their start time. The feed API filtered out anything older than 24 hours. This commit removes that filter and sorts upcoming events first, then recent past events.
backgroundDeep: The app stores shared events in InsForge Postgres. Ingest writes rows via POST; the feed reads them via GET /api/events → listFeedEvents().
backgroundNarrow: fetchUpcomingEventRows() used .gte('start_at', now - 24h). Once an event's start time was more than a day ago, every feed reload hid it — even though the row still existed.
goal: If someone adds an event to the shared feed, it should stay visible until an admin deletes it — not vanish because a server-side date window expired.
figure: Postgres row→API query→Feed UI
intuition: Persistence was fine; the read filter made it look like data loss. Remove the cutoff, then sort so upcoming events appear first (nearest first) and past events follow (most recent first).

steps.label: Remove the 24-hour cutoff
steps.body: fetchAllEventRows() selects all events without .gte('start_at', cutoff), then applies sortFeedEventRows.

steps.label: Sort upcoming first
steps.body: sortFeedEventRows splits on start_at >= now: upcoming events nearest-first, then past events most-recent-first.

steps.label: Regression test
steps.body: Integration test inserts an event from 3 days ago and asserts listFeedEvents() still returns it.

quiz.q: Why did events seem to disappear even though they were in the database?
options: The API excluded rows with start_at older than 24 hours
Events were stored only in sessionStorage
A nightly cleanup job deleted them
feedback: ❌ RLS allows public read; the filter was in application code.
✅ The query used .gte('start_at', cutoff) with a rolling 24h window.
❌ No TTL or cron cleanup exists.

quiz.q: How are feed results ordered after this fix?
options: Upcoming first (nearest first), then past events (most recent first)

quiz.q: Which function name changed to reflect the new behavior?
feedback: ❌ listFeedEvents still exists; it now delegates to listAllFeedEvents.
✅ The internal fetch helper was renamed.

quiz.q: Does this commit change how admin delete works?
options: No — only the feed query and sort changed
feedback: ❌ That was the bug we fixed.

quiz.q: When is the understanding layer generated for this PR?
options: Once, after the PR is created and the feature is production-ready
feedback: ✅ Goal method: implement → review loop → PR → understanding.
❌ Generated on the feature branch before merge.
